provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Urban Planning (Fees) Regulations, 2018.

provisions.title: Fees
provisions.content: The fees specified in the First and Second Schedules to these Regulations shall be payable for the several matters to which they are applicable. First Schedule (Made under regulation 2) Fee and charges for inspection and purchase of approved schmes Inspection of approved detailed planning scheme view and supply charges Inspection____________10,000 Purchase of a printed hard copy Size Black and white Coloured Sepia A4 Copy(text per Page) 1,000 2,000 - A3 Copy (text per Page) 1,000 2,000 - A4 Copy (Map Per Page) 2,000/= 5,000/= 10,000/= A3 Copy (Map Per Page) 5,000/= 10,000/= 20,000/= A2 Copy (Map Per Page) 10,000/= 15,000/= 30,000/= A1 Copy (Map Per Page) 20,000/= 30,000/= 60,000/= A0 Copy (Map Per Page) 20,0000/= 50,000/= 100,000/= Purchase of Soft/CD/DVD copy (if available)_________________________________ 50,000/- General planning scheme Inspection_________________________________20,000/= Second Schedule (Made under regulation 2) S/N Item Fee (Tshs.) 1. Application for planning consent 20,000/= 2. Application to amend a planning consent 20,000/= 3. Application for renewal of a planning consent 20,000/= 4. Application for approval to subdivide land 30,000/= 5. Application to change use of land 30,000/= 6. Application to inspect the urban planning register 5,000/= 7. Application for consent for carrying out work in conservation area 30,000/= 8. Any other application/matter not specifically provided for 30,000/= History of this document 09 March 2018 this version Published in Gazette of the United Republic of Tanzania Commenced 14 February 2018 Assented to Related documents More documents like this one [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all. Free Access to the law supports the rule of law and access to justice. TanzLII partners with African Legal Information Institute and Laws.Africa , who provide it with technical support.
